description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 90.

Heroic Path: Herald

Prerequisites: Bard, Cleric, Paladin, or Warlock Tier I: Voice

Irrepressible Voice. You can choose to make your voice be heard up to 300 feet, and it can be understood regardless of language. You cannot be magically silenced or otherwise prevented from speaking unless you are paralyzed, stunned, or unconscious. Magic cannot compel your speech in any way.

Proclamation. As an action, you speak a proclamation tied to your deity. You can also come up with your own proclamation, with a similar effect, at the DM’s discretion.

Allies who can hear you gain temporary hit points equal to your level and advantage on their next attack roll or saving throw.

Creatures of your choice that can hear you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be affected by the Command spell.

An ongoing 5th-level or lower spell or magical effect of your choice within 30 feet of you ends. +

Heroic Perk: Divine Arrival

Prerequisite: Voice

You can choose to make yourself known whenever you enter a settlement, battlefield, or planar domain. The range at which you can announce yourself is an amount of miles equal to your total character level. Creatures instinctively know something important and divine has arrived. You can force creatures of CR 5 or lower to flee, gather near your position, or pray at your coming. The DM determines exactly how creatures respond to your arrival based on their nature, disposition and your intent. Tier II: Omen Incarnate

Prerequisite: Divine Arrival

Power of the Voice. Your spell save DC is increased by 2 for spells that have verbal components.

Edict. As an action, you can speak a single sentence aloud, dictating a new rule of creation. Choose one of the following effects that applies in a 60-foot radius centered on yourself for the next minute. You can also come up with your own edict, with a similarly powerful effect, at the DM’s discretion. Once you use this ability, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Edict of Bane: Creatures of your choice have disadvantage on all saving throws. +

Edict of Sanctuary: Allies gain resistance to all damage. They gain immunity to damage types they were already resistant to.

Edict of Forbiddance: A specific course of action (fleeing, lying, attacking a specific creature) cannot be performed. If a creature attempts to take the action regardless, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or take 10d6 psychic damage and be unable to take their desired action. Creatures that succeed on this saving throw become immune to your Edict of Forbiddance for the next 24 hours.

Heroic Perk: Words Beyond Worlds

Prerequisite: Omen Incarnate

You ignore material and somatic components when casting spells. As a bonus action, you can send a mental message to a creature of your choice, regardless of distance and even across planes of existence. The creature hears a message of your choice in its mind, recognizes you as the sender if it knows you, and can answer in a like manner immediately if you choose to allow it. This feature enables creatures with Intelligence scores of at least 3 to understand the meaning of your message. Creatures that don’t consider you an enemy are motivated to heed your message and its intent– warnings will be taken seriously, and requests will be acquiesced unless exceedingly difficult or costly to their recipient. Tier III: Herald

Prerequisite: Words Beyond Worlds

Immortal Echo. You are immune to the frightened and charmed conditions. If you fall unconscious or die but your body is not destroyed, your voice continues to echo, allowing you to speak and cast spells as if you were conscious until the end of your next turn. If you are dying, this effect also stabilizes you at the end of that turn.

Word of Creation. As an action, you speak a word of creation, replicating the effects of any spell of 9th level or lower that you are familiar with. Once you use this ability, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Path Connections

Sovereign. Instead of the Divine Arrival Heroic Perk, you can take the Greater Charms Perk, changing to the Sovereign path.

Overlord. Instead of the Divine Arrival Heroic Perk, you can take the Thrall Perk, changing to the Overlord path.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
